name: Hunter's Mark
description: You choose a creature you can see within range and mystically mark it as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 damage to the target whenever you hit it with a weapon attack, and you have advantage on any Perception or Survival check to find it. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this spell ends, you can use a bonus action to mark a new creature.
effect: Marks a target as quarry: +1d6 damage on each weapon hit; advantage on tracking/perceiving the marked creature.
notes: Range: D&D 5e is 90 ft (6 grid); set 2 grid (30 ft) for L1 range balance.
Bonus-action target-switch on kill requires a conditional trigger engine mechanic — see proposals.
Stub had bone-dust×2+pollen — both focus-provided (totem/sprig-of-mistletoe), not a real cost for rangers. Divination tracking mark is not substance-themed. Correctly free.
